name: Neal Hefti
description: American jazz trumpeter, composer, songwriter, and arranger (1922-2008)
abstract: Neal Paul Hefti (October 29, 1922 – October 11, 2008) was an American jazz trumpeter, composer, and arranger. He wrote music for The Odd Couple movie and TV series and for the Batman TV series.

about_quotes.speaker: Jillean Williams
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only responds to the interviewer's narrative about Neal Hefti and Count Basie; no personal interaction or relationship between Jillean Williams and Neal Hefti is described.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jillean Williams interviewed by Milt Fillius and Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 20, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Two of my close friends, Neal Hefti, and Johnny Mandel even more than Neal because I sort of grew up with Johnny, he was my roommate on the Buddy Rich band
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when I was real young, I mean 16 years old, I played in a band called Bob Astor, and my roommate was Neal Hefti.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: And we remain to this day very dear friends. In fact I just spoke to him the day before yesterday.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: We had great trumpet players, but there wasn't much for them to play... Neal Hefti and Bernie Previn had Bop City, then those guys left... We had some good players on that band... Neal Hefti
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: And in the trumpet section was Neil Hefti, Sonny Berman, and God © Fillius Jazz Archive -4- I can’t remember — that’s what happens when you get old, you can’t remember the guys.
